description: WP-69/65 — versioned assistant eval corpus. Golden STRUCTURED expectations (entity id-sets for mutations; row/claim rubric for answers), NEVER exact prose. Runs two ways: through MockInterestAssistant as a deterministic XCTest (CI), and through the REAL FoundationModelsInterestAssistant on an AI-enabled Mac/iPhone via RealFMEvalTests / the DEBUG eval screen. `clock` is the fixed `now` CI evaluates the answer arm against; the on-device run uses the live feed and real time. `knownGap` cases are EXPECTED to fail until the named work package lands — CI skips them (marked), the device run reports them as known gaps. SPORT-LEVEL vs SPECIFIC ENTITY (WP-65 principle): when the user names a BARE sport ('golf', 'litt F1', 'mer sykkel', 'skiskyting') the correct grounding is the SPORT-LEVEL entity (sport-golf, sport-f1, sport-cycling, sport-biathlon) — following the whole sport, not an arbitrary flagship tournament (grounding 'golf' to one random event is wrong). When the user names a SPECIFIC tournament/team/athlete ('Tour de France', 'Lyn', 'Hovland') the specific entity is correct. An umbrella term ('all vintersport') grounds to the single category entity (category-winter-sports). Sports with no published sport entity (tennis, athletics) fall back to their most-headline real entity.

cases.utterance: Følg Casper Ruud bare i Grand Slams

cases.note: Bare sport word → the SPORT-LEVEL entity (WP-65 principle). Re-calibrated from tour-de-france-2026: 'mer sykkel' means the whole sport, not one flagship race.
A SPECIFIC named tournament → the specific entity (not sport-cycling).
Free-text entity NOT in the index → grounding rejects it → nothing applied. The honest-rejection contract: an empty grounded set is the correct outcome.

cases.utterance: Følg Tour de France med fokus på norske utøvere
cases.note: The lens case (WP-16.1). Scored on the entity id-set only; the Norwegian lens itself is covered by MutationGrounderTests.
Bare sport + a Norwegian-focus lens. Sport-level entity (WP-65); the ', bare de norske' tail is a lens modifier, not a second clause.

cases.utterance: Følg Viktor Hovland og Casper Ruud
cases.note: Two explicit index entities in one utterance — the id-set must contain BOTH (a clause dropped silently is the bug WP-65 guards against).

cases.utterance: Følg Magnus Carlsen og Karsten Warholm
Følg FK Lyn Oslo og Magnus Carlsen
Jeg liker golf, spesielt Hovland, og all vintersport, og følger Brann og litt F1
cases.note: The owner's real utterance class (WP-65). FIVE clauses → five proposals: golf→sport-golf, Hovland→viktor-hovland, all vintersport→category-winter-sports, Brann→NOT in the index (honestly reported as not-found, NOT dropped), litt F1→sport-f1. The grounded id-set is the four that resolve; Brann is the per-clause 'fant ikke' the tally reports.

cases.utterance: Jeg følger Viktor Hovland og Casper Ruud, og vil gjerne ha med litt sjakk og F1 også
cases.note: Declarative «jeg følger …» phrasing routed to the mutation arm (WP-65), mixing named athletes with bare sports. Bare 'sjakk'/'F1' → sport-level entities (WP-65 principle).
Two bare sports → two sport-level entities.

cases.utterance: Følg Viktor Hovland, Magnus Carlsen og Casper Ruud
cases.note: Three explicit athletes, comma + og — none may be dropped.
Declarative cue + two bare sports → two sport-level entities.
A specific team + a bare sport in one utterance.

cases.utterance: Følg Tour de France og Magnus Carlsen
cases.note: A specific tournament + a specific athlete — both specific, both kept.

cases.utterance: Jeg følger Viktor Hovland, Casper Ruud og Karsten Warholm
cases.note: Declarative phrasing with three athletes across comma + og.
Two bare winter sports → two sport-level entities (WP-64 winter coverage + WP-65 fan-out).
One resolvable team + one entity NOT in the index. The grounded set is just the team; cricket is the per-clause 'fant ikke' — a clause the tally reports, never silently drops.
The umbrella term grounds to ONE broad-scope category following (WP-64 lands the category entity; WP-65 finalizes the expectation). No longer a known gap.
A single broadly-followed winter sport → its sport-level entity (WP-64). No longer a known gap.

A PRESENTATION filter, not a follow — «vis bare golf denne uka» should temporarily filter the agenda, not add a golf follow. Until WP-67 lands the present-intent arm, the assistant mis-reads it as a follow (grounds sport-golf), so this is a documented known gap: the empty golden marks the correct future outcome (no profile mutation).

cases.utterance: Når er neste Tour de France?
cases.note: Entity-resolve branch — the next matching agenda row for a resolved tournament. Must cite a real cycling row.
Sport-keyword branch — what's coming up in a whole sport.
The «i kveld» calendar question. An empty evening is a legitimate honest answer, so no row count is required — but the reply must be about tonight and must not invent rows.
The assistant answers over the LOCAL agenda (when/where), never past results. It must not fabricate an outcome — the forbidden-claims guard is the real-FM signal here.
